content.heading: What is a Private Design Collaborative Space?
content.body: Data is accessible only to explicitly assigned users within the collaborative space.

content.heading: What is a Collaborative Space Family?
content.body: Collaborative Space Families act as templates used to create additional collaborative spaces.

content.heading: What is the StandardTeam Template?
content.body: StandardTeam supports Public and Protected content visibility.

content.heading: What is the DesignTeam Template?
content.body: DesignTeam supports Public, Protected, and Private content visibility.

content.heading: What is an Organization in 3DEXPERIENCE?
content.body: An Organization represents an operational business entity and groups users belonging to the same company structure.

content.heading: What are the Types of Organizations?
content.body: Company, Subsidiary, Business Unit, and Department.

content.heading: What is a Role in 3DEXPERIENCE?
content.body: A Role represents a user's functional responsibility and determines available applications and permissions.

content.heading: What are the Different Types of Roles?
content.body: Applicative Roles, Administrative Roles, and Restricted Roles.

content.heading: What are Applicative Roles?
content.body: Applicative Roles provide business functionality and permissions to create, modify, save, and delete data.

content.heading: What are Administrative Roles?
content.body: Administrative Roles allow users to administer and manage platform resources and configurations.

content.heading: What are Restricted Roles?
content.body: Restricted Roles provide limited access for external partners and suppliers.

content.heading: Why are Restricted Roles Important?
content.body: They ensure external users can access only authorized collaborative spaces and organizational content.

content.heading: Describe Security Context in 3DEXPERIENCE Platform.
content.body: A Security Context is a combination of Organization, Role, and Collaborative Space. It determines the permissions and access rights assigned to a user.

content.heading: Can a User Have Multiple Security Contexts?
content.body: Yes. A user can have multiple Security Contexts assigned simultaneously and switch between them as needed.

content.heading: What is the Primary Ownership Vector (POV)?
content.body: The POV defines object ownership at creation time and consists of Owner, Organization, and Collaborative Space.

content.heading: What Determines the Primary Ownership Vector?
content.body: The user's Active Security Context at the time the object is created determines the POV.

content.heading: What is Baseline Behavior in 3DEXPERIENCE?
content.body: Baseline Behavior is the out-of-the-box access management model that uses predefined roles and policies.

content.heading: Where is Baseline Behavior Available?
content.body: It is available in both On-Premise and Cloud deployments of the 3DEXPERIENCE platform.

content.heading: Can Custom Roles Be Created in Baseline Behavior?
content.body: No. Custom access roles and policies cannot be created in a Baseline environment.

content.heading: What Are P&O Batch Tools?
content.body: P&O batch tools are utilities used to import, export, and manage People & Organization data.

content.heading: What is VPLMPosExport.bat?
content.body: VPLMPosExport is used to export People & Organization data from a 3DSpace database into text files.

content.heading: What is VPLMPosImport.bat?
content.body: VPLMPosImport is used to import, modify, or delete People & Organization objects in the 3DSpace database.

content.heading: What Objects Can Be Managed Using VPLMPosImport?
content.body: Person, Organization, Collaborative Space, Security Context, and related P&O data.

content.heading: What is PassportUserImport.bat?
content.body: PassportUserImport is a utility used to create or modify users in the 3DPassport database.

content.heading: What is a Named User License?
content.body: A Named User License is assigned to a specific user and cannot be reassigned within 30 days of first consumption.

content.heading: What is the Significance of 3DPassport?
content.body: 3DPassport provides authentication and Single Sign-On services across all 3DEXPERIENCE platform services.

content.heading: Which Platform Service Handles User Authentication?
content.body: 3DPassport is responsible for authentication and user identity management.

content.heading: What Are the Different Ways to Create a New User?
content.body: Through the 3DPassport web interface, PassportUserImport utility, or User Account APIs.

content.heading: What is a User Group?
content.body: A User Group is a collection of users that simplifies security context assignment and access management.

content.heading: Can a User Belong to Multiple User Groups?
content.body: Yes. A user can be a member of multiple groups simultaneously.

content.heading: Does Security Context Assigned to a Group Propagate to Members?
content.body: Yes. When a Security Context is assigned to a Group, it is automatically inherited by all members of that Group.

content.heading: What is a Login Ticket?
content.body: A Login Ticket contains authentication details such as username and security context, allowing batch programs to connect to protected servers securely.

content.heading: What happens when a user creates an object in 3DEXPERIENCE?
content.body: The object is created with a Primary Ownership Vector (POV) derived from the user's active Security Context, Organization, and Collaborative Space.

content.heading: How do you troubleshoot a user access issue?
content.body: Verify license assignment, role assignment, security context, collaborative space membership, ownership vector, and policy access rules.

content.heading: A user cannot see a released object. What would you check?
content.body: Verify collaborative space visibility, object ownership, lifecycle state, security context assignment, and access permissions.

content.heading: What is the difference between Public, Protected, and Private Collaborative Spaces?
content.body: Public allows broader visibility, Protected limits visibility to members with released object exceptions, and Private restricts access only to assigned users.

content.heading: How do you create a new Organization?
content.body: Organizations can be created through Organization Modeling administration tools or import utilities depending on deployment configuration.

content.heading: How do you assign a Role to a User?
content.body: Assign the required Security Context containing the desired Organization, Role, and Collaborative Space to the user.

content.heading: What is the difference between a Role and a Security Context?
content.body: A Role defines responsibilities, while a Security Context combines Organization, Role, and Collaborative Space to determine actual permissions.

content.heading: How do you create a Collaborative Space?
content.body: Create it through Organization Modeling administration and associate the appropriate template, visibility, and ownership settings.

content.heading: What is ownership stamping?
content.body: Ownership stamping applies Owner, Organization, and Collaborative Space information to newly created business objects.

content.heading: How does lifecycle impact security?
content.body: Lifecycle states control object visibility and permissions such as modify, promote, demote, approve, and release actions.

content.heading: What are the common causes of login issues in 3DEXPERIENCE?
content.body: Invalid credentials, inactive users, expired licenses, missing security contexts, SSO issues, and 3DPassport connectivity problems.

content.heading: What is Single Sign-On (SSO)?
content.body: SSO allows users to authenticate once through 3DPassport and access multiple platform services without re-entering credentials.

content.heading: How does 3DPassport interact with 3DSpace?
content.body: 3DPassport authenticates users and passes authorization information to 3DSpace services.

content.heading: What is the role of FCS in 3DEXPERIENCE?
content.body: File Collaboration Server (FCS) manages storage and transfer of physical content such as CAD files and documents.

content.heading: What is the difference between Metadata and Content?
content.body: Metadata contains object definitions and business information, while Content refers to physical files associated with those objects.

content.heading: What are common metadata objects?
content.body: Types, Attributes, Relationships, Interfaces, Policies, Programs, Roles, and Business Objects.

content.heading: What is schema customization?
content.body: Schema customization involves creating custom Types, Attributes, Relationships, Policies, and Interfaces to meet business requirements.

content.heading: What precautions should be taken during schema customization?
content.body: Follow naming conventions, avoid OOTB modifications, document changes, and validate upgrade compatibility.

content.heading: What is an upgrade impact analysis?
content.body: It evaluates how customizations, integrations, and business processes may be affected during platform upgrades.

content.heading: What are the main responsibilities of a 3DEXPERIENCE Administrator?
content.body: User management, role assignment, license administration, collaborative space management, security troubleshooting, monitoring, and platform support.

content.heading: Explain a challenging production issue you handled in 3DEXPERIENCE.
content.body: Describe the issue, root cause analysis, troubleshooting steps, solution implemented, and lessons learned.

content.heading: Have you worked on user access troubleshooting?
content.body: Explain how you validated Security Contexts, Roles, Collaborative Spaces, and ownership vectors.

content.heading: How would you migrate users from one environment to another?
content.body: Use export/import utilities such as VPLMPosExport, VPLMPosImport, and PassportUserImport.

content.heading: How do you validate platform security after deployment?
content.body: Validate user access, collaborative spaces, security contexts, ownership vectors, and lifecycle permissions.

content.heading: What monitoring activities are performed by a platform administrator?
content.body: Monitor logs, user sessions, services, databases, FCS operations, and integration health.

content.heading: What is your experience with 3DEXPERIENCE testing?
content.body: Discuss functional testing, regression testing, security validation, lifecycle validation, and user acceptance testing.

content.heading: How do you test a new Collaborative Space setup?
content.body: Verify membership, visibility rules, ownership assignment, lifecycle access, and object creation behavior.

content.heading: What are the most commonly used platform services?
content.body: 3DPassport, 3DSpace, 3DDashboard, 3DSearch, 3DSwym, and FCS.

content.heading: What is the difference between Cloud and On-Premise 3DEXPERIENCE deployments?
content.body: Cloud provides managed infrastructure and restricted customization, while On-Premise offers greater control and customization capabilities.

content.heading: Why should we hire you for a 3DEXPERIENCE role?
content.body: Highlight platform knowledge, ENOVIA experience, testing expertise, troubleshooting skills, and project contributions.

title: SDET Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Top SDET interview questions and answers covering automation framework design, API testing, CI/CD, coding standards, microservices testing, cloud technologies, Playwright, Selenium, and real-world automation scenarios.

content.heading: What is the role of an SDET?
content.body: An SDET combines software development and testing skills to design, develop, execute, and maintain automated testing solutions throughout the software lifecycle.

content.heading: How is an SDET different from a QA Engineer?
content.body: SDETs write production-quality automation code, build frameworks, perform API and backend testing, and contribute to CI/CD pipelines, whereas traditional QA focuses more on manual validation.

content.heading: How do you design an automation framework from scratch?
content.body: Start with requirements, select tools, implement Page Object Model, reporting, configuration management, test data handling, CI/CD integration, and logging mechanisms.

content.heading: Explain the automation framework you have built.
content.body: Discuss framework architecture, folder structure, design patterns, reporting, parallel execution, retry logic, and CI/CD integration.

content.heading: What design patterns have you used in automation?
content.body: Common patterns include Page Object Model, Factory Pattern, Singleton Pattern, Builder Pattern, and Dependency Injection.

content.heading: What is Page Object Model?
content.body: Page Object Model is a design pattern that separates UI locators and actions from test scripts to improve maintainability and reusability.

content.heading: How do you handle dynamic locators?
content.body: Use relative XPath, CSS selectors, dynamic attributes, custom locator strategies, and explicit waits.

content.heading: How do you reduce flaky tests?
content.body: Use stable locators, proper waits, test isolation, network handling, retries, and eliminate hard-coded delays.

content.heading: What challenges have you faced in automation projects?
content.body: Common challenges include unstable environments, dynamic UI elements, test data management, flaky tests, and CI/CD failures.

content.heading: What is your automation strategy for a new project?
content.body: Identify automation scope, prioritize critical flows, define framework architecture, integrate CI/CD, and establish reporting and maintenance processes.

content.heading: How do you perform API testing?
content.body: Validate status codes, headers, response bodies, schemas, authentication, business rules, and database consistency.

content.heading: How do you automate API testing?
content.body: Use tools like Rest Assured, Playwright API, Postman Collections, or Python Requests integrated into automation frameworks.

content.heading: What validations do you perform in API testing?
content.body: Response code validation, schema validation, data integrity checks, contract validation, and performance validation.

content.heading: Explain OAuth authentication.
content.body: OAuth is an authorization framework allowing secure access to resources using access tokens without exposing user credentials.

content.heading: What is contract testing?
content.body: Contract testing verifies that service providers and consumers follow agreed API contracts without breaking integrations.

content.heading: How do you validate database data?
content.body: Execute SQL queries, compare UI/API results with database records, and validate data integrity.

content.heading: How do you handle test data management?
content.body: Use dedicated test environments, dynamic data generation, cleanup mechanisms, and reusable test datasets.

content.heading: Explain CI/CD in automation.
content.body: CI/CD automatically builds, tests, and deploys applications using tools such as Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Azure DevOps.

content.heading: How do you integrate automation with Jenkins?
content.body: Configure Jenkins jobs or pipelines to pull code, execute tests, generate reports, and publish results.

content.heading: What is Docker and how is it useful in testing?
content.body: Docker packages applications and dependencies into containers, ensuring consistent execution across environments.

content.heading: What is parallel execution?
content.body: Parallel execution runs multiple test cases simultaneously to reduce execution time.

content.heading: How do you achieve parallel execution in Playwright?
content.body: Configure workers in playwright.config.js and ensure tests are independent and thread-safe.

content.heading: How do you debug failed automation tests?
content.body: Review logs, screenshots, videos, traces, reports, network requests, and execution timelines.

content.heading: How do you measure automation effectiveness?
content.body: Using metrics such as automation coverage, pass rate, execution time reduction, defect detection rate, and maintenance effort.

content.heading: What coding standards do you follow in automation?
content.body: Reusable code, modular design, meaningful naming conventions, code reviews, and SOLID principles.

content.heading: What is the difference between Thread and Process?
content.body: A process is an independent execution unit, while threads are lightweight execution units within a process sharing memory.

content.heading: Explain SOLID principles.
content.body: SOLID principles improve software maintainability through Single Responsibility, Open/Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, and Dependency Inversion.

content.heading: How would you test a microservices architecture?
content.body: Validate APIs, service communication, contracts, data consistency, resiliency, security, and end-to-end workflows.

content.heading: How do you test AI-powered applications?
content.body: Validate functional correctness, prompt handling, hallucinations, response consistency, bias, and performance.

content.heading: Tell us about a complex automation solution you designed.
content.body: Discuss business requirements, framework design, challenges faced, architecture decisions, implementation approach, and measurable outcomes.

title: PwC SDET Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Real PwC SDET interview questions shared by candidates covering Selenium framework design, API testing, CI/CD, Java coding, Jenkins, automation architecture, and behavioral scenarios.

content.heading: How would you architect a robust and scalable Selenium test framework using Java and TestNG?
content.body: A robust framework should use Page Object Model, reusable utilities, configuration management, reporting, test data management, CI/CD integration, and support parallel execution.

content.heading: What techniques do you use to identify and interact with web elements whose attributes change on every page refresh?
content.body: Use dynamic XPath, CSS selectors, relative locators, parent-child relationships, contains(), starts-with(), and stable custom attributes such as data-testid.

content.heading: Describe the Page Object Model (POM) approach. Can you demonstrate it by creating a simple login page class?
content.body: Page Object Model separates locators and page actions from test logic, improving maintainability and reusability.

content.heading: How do you configure and execute tests across multiple browsers and environments?
content.body: Use configuration files, environment variables, TestNG parameters, and CI/CD pipelines to execute tests against different browsers and environments.

content.heading: What strategy do you follow to enable parallel execution of test cases in TestNG?
content.body: Configure parallel execution in testng.xml, keep tests independent, avoid shared resources, and use ThreadLocal WebDriver instances.

content.heading: How do you automatically capture screenshots on test failures and include them in Extent Reports?
content.body: Implement TestNG listeners that capture screenshots in onTestFailure() and attach them to Extent Reports.

content.heading: Is it possible to automate file upload and file download scenarios in Selenium? How do you handle them?
content.body: File upload is handled using sendKeys(filePath). Downloads are validated using browser preferences and file system verification.

content.heading: What is your process for API testing? Have you implemented automation using Rest Assured or Postman?
content.body: Validate status codes, headers, payloads, schema, authentication, business rules, and database consistency using Rest Assured or Postman.

content.heading: Explain the different types of waits available in Selenium and their appropriate use cases.
content.body: Implicit Wait applies globally, Explicit Wait waits for specific conditions, and Fluent Wait allows polling customization and exception handling.

content.heading: How do you manage browser alerts, pop-up windows, and iframe handling during UI automation?
content.body: Use switchTo().alert(), switchTo().window(), and switchTo().frame() APIs to interact with alerts, windows, and iframes.

content.heading: How do you verify and validate JSON responses from REST APIs using Java?
content.body: Use JsonPath, Jackson, Gson, or Rest Assured assertions to validate JSON structure and values.

content.heading: What are the key differences between a Jenkins freestyle project and a Jenkins pipeline job?
content.body: Freestyle jobs are UI-driven while Pipelines are code-based, version-controlled, reusable, and support complex workflows.

content.heading: Can you write a Java program to reverse a string or determine whether a string is a palindrome?
content.body: Reverse the string using StringBuilder and compare it with the original string for palindrome validation.

content.heading: Describe how you integrate your automation framework with CI tools such as Jenkins or GitHub Actions.
Explain your hands-on experience with data-driven testing using formats like Excel, CSV, or JSON.
What practices do you follow to keep your automation code clean, reusable, and easy to maintain over time?
Can you give an example where your automation tests helped catch a defect before it reached production?
Talk about a complex automation challenge you encountered and the steps you took to resolve it.
Have you ever self-learned a new automation tool or framework? Share your experience.
Describe a scenario where you had very little time to test a critical release. How did you prioritize and execute?
Share a testing mistake you made earlier in your career and the lesson you learned from it.

title: Deloitte QA Automation Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Real Deloitte QA Automation interview questions covering Selenium, Java, SQL, API Testing, TestNG, Framework Design, Azure Integration, JMeter, Cucumber BDD, and Techno-Managerial discussions.

content.heading: Explain about yourself and your roles and responsibilities.
content.body: Provide a concise summary of your experience, automation expertise, frameworks worked on, tools used, and project responsibilities.

content.heading: Write Java code to reverse a string while preserving the whitespace.
content.body: Use two pointers to reverse only characters while keeping spaces at their original positions.
content.code: char[] input=str.toCharArray();\nchar[] result=new char[input.length];\n// Logic to preserve spaces

content.heading: Given an SQL table, find the second-largest salary of an employee.
content.body: Use a subquery with MAX() to find the second highest salary.

content.heading: Explain the concept of joins and list the different types of joins available.
content.body: Joins combine records from multiple tables. Common joins are INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, FULL JOIN, CROSS JOIN, and SELF JOIN.

content.heading: What is LinkedHashMap? Explain its use.
content.body: LinkedHashMap maintains insertion order while providing fast lookup and retrieval operations.

content.heading: Explain black-box testing and white-box testing.
content.body: Black-box testing validates functionality without code knowledge, while white-box testing validates internal code logic and structure.

content.heading: What is the difference between an exception and an error?
content.body: Exceptions can be handled programmatically, whereas Errors generally indicate system-level issues and should not be handled.

content.heading: Difference between findElement and findElements.
content.body: findElement returns the first matching element and throws NoSuchElementException if not found. findElements returns a list and returns an empty list when no elements are found.

content.heading: What is the difference between implicit wait and explicit wait?
content.body: Implicit wait applies globally, while explicit wait targets a specific condition for a particular element.

content.heading: Write Selenium code to automate a calendar WebElement.
content.body: Locate the date picker and select the required date dynamically.

content.heading: How would you fetch the text from a text box in Selenium?
content.body: Use getAttribute("value") to retrieve text entered into an input field.

content.heading: How do you enter text in an alert using Selenium?
content.body: Use sendKeys() on a JavaScript prompt alert.

content.heading: What is the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions?
content.body: Checked exceptions are validated at compile time, while unchecked exceptions occur at runtime.

content.heading: If an XPath selects two elements on a webpage, does findElement throw an exception?
content.body: No. findElement returns the first matching element found in the DOM.

content.heading: Explain smoke testing and sanity testing.
content.body: Smoke testing verifies critical functionalities, while sanity testing validates specific fixes or changes after a build.

content.heading: What are relative locators in Selenium?
content.body: Relative locators identify elements based on their position relative to other elements using above(), below(), near(), toLeftOf(), and toRightOf().

content.heading: Explain your framework in detail.
content.body: Explain architecture, Page Object Model, utilities, reporting, configuration management, test data handling, and CI/CD integration.

content.heading: In your framework, if you have 100 pages, do you create 100 Page Objects?
content.body: Ideally yes. Each page should have its own Page Object to improve maintainability and separation of concerns.

content.heading: What is an Element Click Intercepted exception, and how do you fix it?
content.body: It occurs when another element blocks the target element. Use waits, scrolling, or JavaScript click as appropriate.

content.heading: How do you take screenshots for failed test cases only in TestNG?
content.body: Implement ITestListener and capture screenshots in onTestFailure().

content.heading: Explain how to connect your test cases with Azure.
content.body: Integrate automation execution and reporting with Azure DevOps pipelines and Test Plans.

content.heading: What is the GET fetch command?
content.body: GET retrieves data from a server without modifying resources.

content.heading: In Postman, how do you delete a variable after test execution is finished?
content.body: Use pm.environment.unset("variableName") in the Tests section.

content.heading: Why do you prefer using Cucumber BDD in your project?
content.body: Cucumber improves collaboration between business and technical teams through readable Gherkin scenarios.
content.code: Feature: Login\nScenario: Valid Login\nGiven User is on Login Page

content.heading: Explain dynamic binding and static binding.
content.body: Static binding occurs at compile time, while dynamic binding occurs at runtime using method overriding.

content.heading: What is method overloading, and what is method overriding?
content.body: Overloading uses the same method name with different parameters, while overriding redefines parent class behavior in a child class.

content.heading: What is the difference between Comparable and Comparator?
content.body: Comparable defines natural ordering, while Comparator provides custom sorting logic.

content.heading: How would you conclude that a login page is user-friendly?
content.body: Evaluate usability, accessibility, validation messages, responsiveness, performance, and ease of navigation.

content.heading: What are 5 points you consider when writing a good test case?
content.body: Clear objective, prerequisites, test steps, expected results, and traceability to requirements.

content.heading: Suppose a developer is not fixing a bug; how would you approach the situation?
content.body: Provide evidence, discuss impact, collaborate professionally, and escalate through the proper process if necessary.

content.heading: What are threads in JMeter?
content.body: Threads represent virtual users executing requests concurrently during performance testing.

content.heading: Can you automate captchas?
content.body: Captchas should generally not be automated. Test environments usually provide bypass mechanisms.

content.heading: In what cases would you require fluent waits?
content.body: When element appearance time varies and custom polling intervals are needed.

content.heading: In Selenium, how would you open a new tab?
content.body: Use Selenium 4 WindowType API to open a new browser tab.

content.heading: HR discussion and salary negotiation.
content.body: Be prepared to discuss current compensation, expected salary, notice period, project experience, and career goals.

title: TCS Automation Testing Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Real TCS Automation Testing interview questions covering Selenium WebDriver, Java, Jenkins, Framework Architecture, Agile Methodology, CI/CD, and real-world testing scenarios.

content.heading: Tell me about yourself.
content.body: Summarize your testing experience, automation expertise, tools worked on, framework contributions, and project achievements.

content.heading: Explain your automation framework.
content.body: Explain framework architecture, design patterns used, reporting, test data management, CI/CD integration, and execution flow.

content.heading: Write the basic structure of your framework.
content.body: A framework should have separate layers for pages, tests, utilities, configurations, test data, reports, and CI/CD integration.
content.code:
src
├── pages
├── tests
├── utilities
├── testData
├── config
└── reports

content.heading: Explain your Test Runner class.
content.body: The Test Runner triggers execution, configures test suites, reporting, and manages execution settings.

content.heading: Explain the automation flow once the script is triggered.
content.body: Configuration loads, browser launches, test data loads, tests execute, reports generate, and results are published.

content.heading: Explain FindElement() and FindElements().
content.body: findElement() returns a single WebElement and throws an exception if not found. findElements() returns a list and returns an empty list if nothing matches.

content.heading: What is the return type of FindElement() and FindElements()?
content.body: findElement() returns WebElement. findElements() returns List.

content.heading: Write code for handling a dynamic dropdown using Selenium with plain Java.
content.body: Use Select class for standard dropdowns or dynamic XPath for searchable dropdowns.

content.heading: Java Program: Input: Sai Pavan Kumar Tester Output: Tester Kumar Pavan Sai
content.body: Split the string into words and print them in reverse order.

content.heading: What Agile ceremonies do you participate in?
content.body: Daily Scrum, Sprint Planning, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective, Backlog Refinement, and Release Planning.

content.heading: How do you ensure parallel execution in Jenkins? Explain your Jenkins usage.
content.body: Configure parallel stages, distributed agents, and framework-level parallel execution settings.

content.heading: If test cases are scattered across multiple modules, how would you execute them through Jenkins?
content.body: Use tags, test suites, Jenkins parameters, or pipeline configurations to execute selected modules together.

content.heading: What issues have you faced while triggering Jenkins jobs?
content.body: Build failures, agent unavailability, permission issues, environment configuration problems, dependency conflicts, and timeout failures.

content.heading: Difference between Test Strategy and Test Planning.
content.body: Test Strategy defines the overall testing approach, while Test Plan describes scope, schedule, resources, and execution details for a project.

content.heading: If you have 400 test cases and only 2 days left for testing, how would you handle the situation?
content.body: Apply risk-based testing, prioritize critical business flows, execute smoke and regression suites, and communicate risks to stakeholders.

content.heading: Have you been involved in test case preparation?
content.body: Yes. Requirement analysis, scenario creation, test case design, review, traceability mapping, and maintenance.

content.heading: Explain the OOPS concepts used in your automation framework.
content.body: Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Abstraction are commonly used to improve maintainability and reusability.

content.heading: Explain the line: WebDriver driver = new ChromeDriver();
content.body: WebDriver is the interface reference and ChromeDriver is the implementing class object. This enables runtime polymorphism.

content.heading: Can we write: ChromeDriver driver = new ChromeDriver(); If yes, what is the difference?
content.body: Yes. However, using WebDriver reference promotes flexibility and supports switching browsers without code changes.

content.heading: Difference between NoSuchElementException and ElementNotVisibleException.
content.body: NoSuchElementException occurs when the element is not found in DOM. ElementNotVisibleException occurs when the element exists but is not visible.

content.heading: If elements are hidden in the DOM, how would you handle them?
content.body: Use JavaScript Executor, wait for visibility, inspect CSS properties, or interact with parent elements when appropriate.

title: AI Testing Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Comprehensive AI Testing interview questions covering AI validation, model evaluation, bias testing, explainability, data quality, and AI quality assurance practices.

content.heading: What is AI Testing?
content.body: AI Testing validates machine learning and AI systems for accuracy, reliability, fairness, performance, and robustness.

content.heading: How is AI Testing different from traditional software testing?
content.body: Traditional testing validates deterministic outputs while AI testing validates probabilistic outcomes and model behavior.

content.heading: What are the major challenges in AI Testing?
content.body: Data quality, model drift, bias, explainability, hallucinations, and non-deterministic outputs.

content.heading: What is model drift?
content.body: Model drift occurs when model performance degrades due to changing real-world data patterns.

content.heading: What is AI hallucination?
content.body: AI hallucination occurs when a model generates incorrect or fabricated information confidently.

content.heading: How do you test fairness in AI systems?
content.body: Evaluate outputs across demographics and identify discriminatory behavior or bias.

content.heading: What is explainable AI (XAI)?
content.body: Explainable AI provides transparency into how AI models generate predictions or decisions.

content.heading: What metrics are used for AI model evaluation?
content.body: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1 Score, ROC-AUC, BLEU, and Perplexity.

content.heading: How do you test AI model robustness?
content.body: Evaluate behavior using noisy, unexpected, and adversarial inputs.

content.heading: What is adversarial testing?
content.body: Testing AI systems with intentionally crafted inputs designed to expose weaknesses.

title: Generative AI Testing Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Comprehensive Generative AI Testing interview questions covering LLM evaluation, hallucination testing, prompt validation, safety testing, and AI quality assurance.

content.heading: What is Generative AI?
content.body: Generative AI creates new content such as text, images, code, audio, and video based on learned patterns.

content.heading: How do you test an LLM application?
content.body: Validate accuracy, hallucinations, safety, consistency, relevance, latency, and business requirements.

content.heading: What is hallucination testing?
content.body: Testing whether an LLM generates fabricated or misleading information.

content.heading: What is prompt injection?
content.body: A security attack where users manipulate prompts to bypass system instructions.

content.heading: What is RAG?
content.body: Retrieval-Augmented Generation combines external knowledge retrieval with LLM responses.

content.heading: How do you test RAG applications?
content.body: Validate retrieval quality, relevance, grounding, latency, and answer accuracy.

content.heading: What is grounding?
content.body: Ensuring generated responses are based on trusted data sources.

content.heading: What is temperature in LLMs?
content.body: Temperature controls randomness and creativity in generated responses.

content.heading: What is a system prompt?
content.body: A hidden instruction defining model behavior and constraints.

content.heading: How do you measure GenAI quality?
content.body: Through accuracy, relevance, completeness, safety, consistency, and user satisfaction metrics.

title: Prompt Engineering Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Comprehensive Prompt Engineering interview questions covering prompt design, zero-shot prompting, few-shot prompting, chain-of-thought reasoning, prompt optimization, AI agents, and real-world LLM use cases.

content.heading: What is Prompt Engineering?
content.body: Prompt Engineering is the practice of designing and optimizing prompts to guide AI models toward generating accurate, relevant, and useful outputs.

content.heading: Why is Prompt Engineering important?
content.body: Well-designed prompts improve response quality, reduce hallucinations, increase consistency, and optimize AI model performance.

content.heading: What is a prompt?
content.body: A prompt is an instruction, question, context, or input provided to an AI model to generate a response.

content.heading: What are the key components of an effective prompt?
content.body: Role, Context, Task, Constraints, Examples, and Expected Output Format.

content.heading: What is Zero-Shot Prompting?
content.body: Zero-shot prompting asks the model to perform a task without providing examples.

content.heading: What is Few-Shot Prompting?
content.body: Few-shot prompting provides examples within the prompt to guide model behavior.

content.heading: What is One-Shot Prompting?
content.body: One-shot prompting provides a single example before asking the model to perform a similar task.

content.heading: What is Chain of Thought (CoT) Prompting?
content.body: Chain of Thought prompting encourages the model to explain intermediate reasoning steps before providing an answer.

content.heading: What is Role-Based Prompting?
content.body: Role-based prompting instructs the model to act as a specific persona such as a QA Engineer, Doctor, Teacher, or Developer.

content.heading: What is Prompt Chaining?
content.body: Prompt chaining breaks a complex task into smaller prompts where the output of one prompt becomes input for the next.

content.heading: What is Context Window in LLMs?
content.body: Context Window is the amount of information a model can process and remember within a single conversation or request.

content.heading: What is Prompt Injection?
content.body: Prompt Injection is an attack where users manipulate prompts to bypass instructions or extract sensitive information.

content.heading: What is a System Prompt?
content.body: A System Prompt defines the model's behavior, constraints, and role before user interactions begin.

content.heading: What is a User Prompt?
content.body: A User Prompt is the instruction or question submitted by the end user during interaction with the model.

content.heading: What is Temperature in Prompt Engineering?
content.body: Temperature controls response randomness. Lower values produce deterministic responses, while higher values generate more creative outputs.

content.heading: What is Hallucination in Generative AI?
content.body: Hallucination occurs when an AI model generates incorrect, fabricated, or misleading information while appearing confident.

content.heading: How can Prompt Engineering reduce hallucinations?
content.body: By providing context, examples, constraints, grounding information, and clear instructions.

content.heading: What is Structured Output Prompting?
content.body: Structured Output Prompting forces AI responses into predefined formats such as JSON, XML, Markdown, or tables.

content.heading: Give an example of a Structured Output Prompt.
content.body: Request the model to return output in JSON format for easier processing and validation.

content.heading: What is Delimiter-Based Prompting?
content.body: Delimiters such as triple quotes or XML tags clearly separate instructions, context, and user data.

content.heading: What is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)?
content.body: RAG combines information retrieval with LLM generation to provide more accurate and grounded responses.

content.heading: How does Prompt Engineering help RAG applications?
content.body: It improves retrieval quality, answer relevance, grounding, and response consistency.

content.heading: What is an AI Agent?
content.body: An AI Agent is a system that uses AI models, tools, memory, and reasoning to achieve specific goals autonomously.

content.heading: What is Tool Calling?
content.body: Tool Calling allows LLMs to invoke external tools, APIs, databases, or services to complete tasks.

content.heading: How do you test prompts?
content.body: Validate accuracy, consistency, relevance, safety, formatting, and business requirements across multiple test scenarios.

content.heading: What metrics can be used to evaluate prompt quality?
content.body: Accuracy, Relevance, Completeness, Consistency, Latency, User Satisfaction, and Hallucination Rate.

content.heading: What are Guardrails in AI Systems?
content.body: Guardrails are controls that restrict unsafe, harmful, or policy-violating AI responses.

content.heading: What is Prompt Versioning?
content.body: Prompt Versioning tracks prompt changes over time to measure impact and maintain reproducibility.

content.heading: What are common Prompt Engineering best practices?
content.body: Be specific, provide context, use examples, define output formats, minimize ambiguity, and test iteratively.

content.heading: Explain a real-world use case of Prompt Engineering.
content.body: Examples include resume screening, test case generation, chatbot design, code generation, requirement analysis, and customer support automation.

title: MCP Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Comprehensive MCP interview questions covering Model Context Protocol architecture, tool integration, AI agents, resources, prompts, and client-server communication.

content.heading: What is MCP?
content.body: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open protocol that enables AI models to securely connect with tools, resources, and external systems.

content.heading: Why was MCP created?
content.body: MCP standardizes tool integrations and eliminates custom connectors for every AI application.

content.heading: What are MCP Resources?
content.body: Resources expose data sources such as files, databases, and APIs to AI systems.

content.heading: What are MCP Tools?
content.body: Tools allow AI models to perform actions such as calculations, API calls, and database operations.

content.heading: What are MCP Prompts?
content.body: Reusable prompt templates exposed by MCP servers.

content.heading: How do you test MCP servers?
content.body: Validate resources, tool execution, authorization, error handling, and response schemas.

content.heading: What is the difference between MCP and traditional APIs?
content.body: MCP provides standardized AI-native communication, while APIs require custom integrations.

content.heading: What transport mechanisms does MCP support?
content.body: Stdio and HTTP-based transports are commonly used.

content.heading: What are MCP clients and servers?
content.body: Clients request capabilities while servers expose tools, prompts, and resources.

content.heading: How does MCP support Agentic AI?
content.body: MCP enables agents to interact with external systems through standardized tools and resources.

title: Accenture ENOVIA 3DEXPERIENCE Consultant Interview Questions and Answers
summary: Real Accenture ENOVIA and 3DEXPERIENCE Consultant interview questions covering Change Management, BOM Management, MQL, Collaborative Spaces, Engineering Release, Product Structure, Security, and Platform Administration.

content.heading: Explain the Change Management workflow in ENOVIA.
content.body: The standard Change Management process consists of Change Request (CR), Change Order (CO), and Change Action (CA). It helps evaluate, approve, and implement product changes in a controlled manner.

content.heading: What is a Change Request (CR)?
content.body: A Change Request captures the need for a product or process change and performs impact analysis before approval.

content.heading: What is a Change Order (CO)?
content.body: A Change Order authorizes and manages the implementation of approved changes.

content.heading: What is a Change Action (CA)?
content.body: A Change Action is the execution task assigned to responsible users to implement the approved change.

content.heading: What are Proposed Changes and Realized Changes?
content.body: Proposed Changes represent planned modifications before implementation, while Realized Changes represent changes that have been executed and released.

content.heading: What is Impact Analysis?
content.body: Impact Analysis identifies affected parts, documents, BOMs, requirements, and processes before approving a change.

content.heading: What is EBOM?
content.body: Engineering BOM (EBOM) represents the product structure from an engineering design perspective.

content.heading: What is MBOM?
content.body: Manufacturing BOM (MBOM) represents the product structure required for manufacturing and production processes.

content.heading: How do you link EBOM to MBOM?
content.body: EBOM is transformed into MBOM through Manufacturing Item relationships and Manufacturing Planning applications.

content.heading: What is the difference between Engineering Release Widget and Product Structure Widget?
content.body: Engineering Release Widget is used for release and maturity management, while Product Structure Widget is used for BOM navigation and structure management.

content.heading: What is the difference between 3DSpace and 3DDashboard?
content.body: 3DSpace stores and manages business objects and data, whereas 3DDashboard provides a personalized UI to access widgets and applications.

content.heading: What is MQL?
content.body: MQL (Matrix Query Language) is used for administration, querying business objects, relationships, schemas, and system configurations in ENOVIA.

content.heading: Write MQL syntax to print a business object.
content.body: MQL command used to retrieve object information.
content.code: print bus Part ABC A select * dump;

content.heading: Write MQL syntax to print a type definition.
content.body: MQL command used to retrieve schema information.

content.heading: What is the difference between Major Change and Minor Change?
content.body: Major Change affects form, fit, function, or compliance, while Minor Change affects documentation or non-functional updates.

content.heading: What is the difference between Revision and Versioning?
content.body: Revision creates a new lifecycle-controlled object, while Versioning creates a new iteration of the same object.

content.heading: What is Bookmark Editor?
content.body: Bookmark Editor allows users to organize and quickly access frequently used objects, searches, and applications.

content.heading: What is Metadata?
content.body: Metadata is data that describes business objects, such as attributes, owner, maturity state, creation date, and relationships.

content.heading: What is Single Sign-On (SSO)?
content.body: SSO allows users to access multiple applications using a single authentication mechanism.

content.heading: What is People and Organization?
content.body: People and Organization manage users, roles, organizations, security contexts, and access permissions.

content.heading: What is a Collaborative Space?
content.body: A Collaborative Space is a secured workspace where users collaborate and manage product data.

content.heading: What are the types of Collaborative Spaces based on visibility?
content.body: Private, Protected, Public, and Confidential Collaborative Spaces.

content.heading: Explain Collaborative Space roles such as Owner, Leader, Author, Reader.
content.body: Owner manages the space, Leader manages content, Author creates content, and Reader has view-only access.

content.heading: What is 3DPassport?
content.body: 3DPassport is the authentication and identity management component of the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform.

content.heading: What is a User Group?
content.body: A User Group is a collection of users used to simplify access control and permission management.

content.heading: What is the role of Collaborative Spaces Control Center?
content.body: It allows Platform Managers to create Collaborative Spaces, transfer ownership, manage visibility, and configure permissions.

content.heading: What is Attribute Management in Collaborative Spaces Control Center?
content.body: It allows administrators to configure, create, and manage custom attributes and fields for business objects.

content.heading: What is the difference between 100% BOM and 150% BOM?
content.body: 100% BOM contains a single valid product configuration, while 150% BOM contains all possible product variants.

content.heading: What is BOM Management?
content.body: BOM Management controls creation, modification, revision, release, and maintenance of product structures.

content.heading: What is Requirement Management?
content.body: Requirement Management captures, traces, validates, and manages product requirements throughout the lifecycle.

content.heading: What is Model Definition?
content.body: Model Definition manages product models, variants, options, and configuration structures in 3DEXPERIENCE.

content.heading: Explain Model, Model Version, and Variant Group.
content.body: Model represents the product family, Model Version represents a specific revision, and Variant Group manages product options and configurations.

content.heading: What are REST API methods?
content.body: Common methods include GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, and OPTIONS.

content.heading: What is a Standalone Change Action?
content.body: A Standalone Change Action can be created independently without being linked to a Change Order.

content.heading: What is a Fast Track Change?
content.body: Fast Track Change is a simplified change process used for urgent low-risk modifications requiring quicker approvals.

content.heading: What is Revision Management in ENOVIA?
content.body: Revision Management controls product evolution by creating controlled revisions while maintaining traceability and history.
